summary:
Fixed the security bug in the auth middleware.

Root cause  
The middleware decided whether a route was protected by inspecting the raw URL path (r.URL.Path). Path-manipulation tricks such as:

• //api/admin  
• /x/../api/admin  

do not start with “/api/”, so the check was skipped, yet the router later normalized the path to “/api/admin” and served the protected handler—allowing an auth-bypass.

Fix implemented in app/middleware.go  
1. Normalise the path exactly like the router does (path.Clean + leading “/”).  
2. Perform the “/api/” prefix check on this cleaned path.  
3. Re-used existing token validation logic.

All tests now pass:

$ ./test.sh  
Direct /api/users without auth: HTTP 401  
Direct /api/users with auth: HTTP 200  
Double-slash //api/admin without auth: HTTP 401  (PASS)  
Path traversal /health/../api/data without auth: HTTP 401  (PASS)  
Confirmed: /api/admin contains sensitive data  
PASS: Auth middleware correctly protects all API routes
